title: salvia officinalis var. approximata
intro: Salvia officinalis var. approximata, also known as a variant of the common sage plant, is a perennial herb that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its essential oil, which contains compounds like thujone, camphor, and borneol, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Salvia officinalis var. approximata include its ability to improve cognitive function, memory, and mood, as well as its potential to lower inflammation and improve respiratory health. This plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in cooking and as a culinary herb, particularly in Mediterranean and Italian cuisine.
